reason: 需 is a bound morpheme and cannot stand alone as a verb; correct form is 需要.

old: His educational background is very high.
new: His level of education is very high.
reason: 'Educational background is very high' is unnatural; 'level of education is very high' is idiomatic English.

old: When she heard the bad news, tears came out.
new: When she heard the bad news, she was moved to tears.
reason: 'Tears came out' is unnatural English; 'moved to tears' or 'tears streamed down' is idiomatic.

old: He bent his waist to pick up the item.
new: He bent down to pick up the item.
reason: 'Bent his waist' is unnatural English; 'bent down' is the natural phrasing.

old: Participating in volunteer service activity is very meaningful.
new: Participating in volunteer service is very meaningful.
reason: 'Service activity' is redundant; 'volunteer service' is sufficient.

old: The significance of this discovery is very important.
reason: 'Significance is very important' is tautological; rewritten to natural English.
用户 means 'user', not 'consumer'; 消费者 is consumer.

old: The number of consumers has increased.
new: The number of users has increased.

old: Our team has the greatest advantage in this competition.
new: Our team has an advantage in this competition.
reason: 'Greatest advantage' overstates the meaning; 优势 means 'advantage/upper hand', not necessarily the greatest.
